subject: docs(todo): flag pwa.js + scripts/ file-size findings from retrospective
body:
The three audit passes focused on `dashboard/js/sections/` and the
shared components in `dashboard/js/components/`. A retrospective
file-size sweep over the whole repo caught three files that the
audit missed:

  - **dashboard/js/pwa.js: 578 lines** (OVER soft-limit 500) — not
    touched by any audit pass because none of them ran a file-size
    sweep over the top-level `dashboard/js/*.js` modules. Has clear
    responsibility boundaries that could be split (install flow,
    update flow, instructions data, event bridge).

  - **scripts/aggregate-processed.js: 1042 lines** (OVER strong-limit
    800) — the active data pipeline for the dashboard. Not touched
    by the audit because `scripts/` was out of scope. Should be split
    via a `scripts/aggregate/` subdirectory with per-concern modules
    (read-commits, summary, time-windows, authors) orchestrated by a
    thin top-level file.

  - **scripts/extract-api.js: 699 lines** (OVER soft-limit 500) —
    same out-of-scope reason. Monitor for growth.

All three added to the existing `File-size monitoring` section in
docs/TODO.md with concrete split suggestions for each. None are
actionable today (the audit batch is closing), but they're now
captured so the next session doesn't re-discover them from scratch.

The existing `useTimelineCharts.js` (416) and `AppContext.jsx` (338)
entries renumbered to make room.

Not a code change — docs only. Build and tests unaffected (63/63
pass from the previous tripwire commit).

Tags: documentation, todo, audit, retrospective
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: added
Epic: post-audit-cleanup
Semver: patch
title: flag pwa.js + scripts/ file-size findings from retrospective
